latest_public_read: April 2026 global semiconductor sales were $110.5B, up 11.0% month-to-month and 93.9% year-to-year.
source: SIA/WSTS April 2026 global semiconductor sales release
as_of: Release date 2026-06-05; monthly sales are a three-month moving average.
investment_use: Use as a top-down cycle gauge and demand-momentum check, not as a company-level revenue proxy.
coverage_implication: Elevates AI-exposed compute, memory, networking, foundry, OSAT, substrate/PCB, optical and power/cooling chains.

latest_public_read: SIA endorsed WSTS Spring 2026 forecast projecting 2026 global semiconductor sales growth of 90% to $1.5T and 2027 sales above $1.9T.
investment_use: Treat as top-down market context; pressure-test against company guidance, channel inventory and pricing.
coverage_implication: Requires separate bull/base/bear logic for AI winners versus lagging auto/industrial/consumer chains.

latest_public_read: SIA/Deloitte reported chips account for more than 95% of a leading AI server rack content value and over 50% of AI data-center capex.
source: SIA/Deloitte AI data-center stack study release
investment_use: Use to justify rack-level value bridge rather than stopping at accelerator revenue.
coverage_implication: For AI coverage, model accelerator + HBM + package/substrate + networking/optical + power/analog + system integration.

latest_public_read: The SIA/Deloitte release states annual revenue from semiconductors used in AI data centers could reach over $1.2T by 2028.
source: SIA/Deloitte AI data-center stack study release
investment_use: Use as a strategic TAM marker, then reconcile to company-specific capacity, share and ASP assumptions.
coverage_implication: High TAM does not remove bottleneck risk; monitor power, HBM, CoWoS, optical and customer ROI.

topic: AI rack chip count and full-stack demand
latest_public_read: The SIA/Deloitte release says a single AI server rack contains over 4,500 packaged chips across logic, memory, analog and foundational technologies.
source: SIA/Deloitte AI data-center stack study release
investment_use: Use to map second- and third-order beneficiaries beyond GPU/ASIC leaders.
coverage_implication: Supports including analog/power, sensors/controllers, passives, interconnect and EMS/ODM in the semi-tech mapping boundary.

latest_public_read: SEMI reported 2025 silicon wafer shipments increased 5.8% to 12,973 MSI while revenue slipped 1.2% to $11.4B.
source: SEMI Silicon Manufacturers Group 2025 year-end release
investment_use: Use as materials-layer cycle check: units can recover while pricing/mix revenue remains soft.
coverage_implication: Separate advanced logic/HBM wafer demand from mature-node recovery and legacy pricing pressure.
